commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh This is my rest forever: here will I dwell; for I have desired it. Augustine of Hippo 15. "This shall be My rest for ever" (ver. These are the words of God. "My rest:" I rest there. How greatly doth God love us, brethren, since, because we rest, He saith that He also resteth. For He is not sometimes Himself disturbed, nor doth He rest as we do; but He saith that He resteth there, because we shall have rest in Him. "Here will I dwell: for I have a delight therein.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
This. God is introduced speaking to the end. (Haydock)
Chosen. Hebrew, "desired. "This relates to his spouse, the Church. (Calmet)
He dwelleth in this Sion for ever. (Worthington)
It was the figure of the Christian Church, as this is of heaven, Hebrews xii. 22. (Berthier)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
